free_text: Zero-degree measurements of 12C fragmentation at
95 MeV/nucleon on thin targets
Previous experiment (E600 experiment)
Carbon    (1.0 mm and 176+/-2 mg/cm2 thick),
CH2       (2.0 mm and 179+/-2 mg/cm2 thick),
Titanium  (0.5 mm and 226+/-2 mg/cm2 thick),
Aluminium (0.7 mm and 177+/-2 mg/cm2 thick).
Two telescopes
1. in the reaction chamber at 9 deg for cross check
with the 9 deg values from the E600 experiment,
2. in the beam axis, at a distance of 768(5) mm
behind the targets
consisting of
thin Si  (146 um thick, 1.954 cm diam.),
thick Si (1 mm thick,   1.954 cm diam.),
CsI      (12 cm thick,  3 cm diam.)
with solid angle of 0.51+/-0.03 msr.
Beam monitor to detect X-rays from Ag foil
(7 um thick).
No information on the source of uncertainty
LPC hadrontherapy web site
(http://hadrontherapy-data.in2p3.fr/)
SB
On. All data sets compiled from Appendices
of J,PR/C,89,064615,2014 deleted. 0 deg DDX on the
LPC hadrontherapy web site inserted (087-098).
